frameworks.name: Bayesian function learning
frameworks.one_line: Infer invariant structural parameters governing asset performance trajectories

frameworks.name: Probabilistic forecasting
frameworks.one_line: Decisions driven by predicted final performance at task horizon

frameworks.name: Optimal foraging
frameworks.one_line: Myopic baseline assuming direct continuation of recent observations

frameworks.name: Study time allocation
frameworks.one_line: Myopic baseline relying solely on recent reward signals

core_question: How do people decide which dynamically-changing asset to invest in or switch away from?
key_assumptions: Assets change endogenously with continued investment
Agents infer invariant structural parameters underlying performance changes
Choices are made by forecasting performance to a task-specific time horizon
manipulations: Statistical properties of asset performance trajectories
key_findings: Bayesian forecasting model consistently outperforms myopic alternatives across four experiments
Behavior is sensitive to time horizon and trajectory statistics
Choices reflect anticipated future performance, not just recent observations
interpretations: Humans use future-looking cognition that anticipates how actions shape future asset performance
Representations of underlying structural dynamics inform present choices
